title: analytics — compliance evidence
description: Primary control points for GDPR (Art. 6, 7, 17, 21, 30, 32), CCPA §1798.140 profiling, and SOC 2 Type 2 (CC7.3, CC8.1). Consent gate + PII-refusal + retention runner are the load-bearing compliance surfaces.

controls.evidence: Every AnalyticsEvent captures a consent_snapshot at CREATE time. Dispatch re-checks consent state against the snapshot. Events for subjects without `analytics` consent are refused (suppressed_by_consent status). Consent trail lives in compliance::ConsentRecord — immutable, tenant + subject + category snapshotted.

controls.principle: right to withdraw consent as easily as it was given
controls.evidence: compliance::ConsentWithdrawn event fires when a subject withdraws — analytics::SuppressPendingEventsOnConsentWithdrawnListener marks any pending events for that subject as suppressed. No further dispatches will fire post-withdrawal for events captured before withdrawal.

user::UserErased cascades analytics::RedactAnalyticsSubjectOnUserErasureListener — redacts subject-identifying properties on events, sets AnalyticsIdentity.identified_user_id=null, hashes device_fingerprint. Aggregate reporting continues from the behavioral shape.

controls.principle: right to object to processing (including profiling)
controls.evidence: Behavioral analytics IS profiling for CCPA purposes. Subject-level opt-out via consent withdrawal (Art. 7(3) mechanism) satisfies Art. 21 objection. AnalyticsEventPolicy view enforces tenant-scoping so no cross-tenant profiling can leak.

PropertyValidator refuses PII in properties + context at CREATE time. IP addresses are hashed at capture boundary (never raw). Device fingerprints are hashed. Attribution is snapshotted immutable so replaying an event later doesn't leak fresh attribution context.
subprocessors.json lists PostHog / Amplitude / Mixpanel / Segment / June / GA4 as Article 28 processors with DPA URLs + certifications. DPAs on file, referenced by DSAR orchestrator.
audit_log rows for every AnalyticsProviderConfig change (credential rotation, sampling change, enabled_event_types change). 7-year retention per foundation's audit tier.

TLS 1.3 for outbound to every provider. Provider webhook signatures verified inbound. AnalyticsProviderConfig.config encrypted at rest AES-256-GCM via Laravel Crypt. Secrets in Doppler; never in code.

Provider region selection via provider config (PostHog EU, Amplitude EU, GA4 measurement_id region). Tenant may pin to EU-only providers by including only EU endpoints in enabled_event_types. Legally: adequacy decisions + SCC clauses on file with each provider.

controls.principle: behavioral profiling categorised as personal information
controls.evidence: Every analytics event contributes to the subject's behavioral profile. Consent snapshot + AnalyticsEvent visibility give the subject standing to invoke §1798.115 right-to-know via the compliance module's DSAR runner (analytics is a DsarExportable contributor).
compliance::UserErased cascade + PurgeAnalyticsDataForErasedTenant handle full erasure. Deletion runs within the compliance module's DSAR SLA (30 days baseline).

Analytics data is NEVER sold. When a subject opts out of sharing (functionally equivalent to withdrawing analytics consent), the consent gate suppresses all future dispatch. Provider configs are per-tenant — no cross-tenant data pooling.

feature-flags.json per-provider kill switches. Emergency ops can flip a bad provider off in seconds without a redeploy. Circuit breakers auto-open on failure rate breach; auto-close on recovery. Alerts fire via AnalyticsProviderCircuitBreakerOpenedNotification within 15 minutes.

metrics.json declares 12 OpenTelemetry metrics covering send volume, delivery latency, failure rates by state, circuit-breaker state, batch flush timing, sampling drift. Alerts fire on threshold breach.

Every AnalyticsProviderConfig change writes an audit row via HasAuditable trait — credential rotation, enabled_event_types adjustment, sampling change, circuit_breaker reset. 7-year retention. Config column encrypted so audit rows don't leak credentials.
Retention runner (PurgeExpiredAnalyticsDataJob weekly) hard-purges past retention window. TenantErased cascade delete complete + audited.
TLS 1.3 outbound + signed inbound webhooks (per provider) + HMAC for custom_webhook driver.
